name: Cascade
description_short: Cascade Community Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution headquartered in Douglas County, Oregon, serving the region for over 75 years.
description_long:
Cascade Community Credit Union began in 1948 as DougCo Schools Credit Union, founded by 15 educators who literally kept their pooled assets in a shoebox locked in a closet. Over decades, the institution merged with other local institutions and expanded to serve all of Douglas County while maintaining its original mission of community-focused financial service. Today, it remains the only financial institution headquartered in Douglas County and ranks as the #1 credit union in Oregon and #3 in the United States by certain metrics.

Cascade offers a full range of consumer financial products including checking accounts, credit cards with rewards and cash back options, auto loans with competitive rates, mortgages (marketed as #1 mortgage lenders), and lines of credit. The credit union emphasizes lower interest rates, more flexible loan terms, and fewer fees compared to traditional banks, which is a standard advantage of member-owned credit union structure. All products are designed with the member-owner model in mind, where profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders.

What distinguishes Cascade is its deep local commitment and community investment. The organization donated $40,000+ to Douglas County schools in 2025, invested $70,000+ in community events, and contributed $40,000+ to 4H and FFA chapters. Members receive exclusive perks including free admission to Wildlife Safari during designated member days, discounted admission to Festival of Lights, and access to sponsored hours at local businesses offering free food and drinks. The credit union actively partners with local auto dealers and offers promotional financing rates throughout the year.

Cascade is well-suited for Douglas County residents and those who work or worship in the region, as membership is geographically restricted. The credit union's strength lies in community integration and local accountability rather than technological innovation or national reach. Members should note that service is limited to specific geographic areas, and the website does not prominently feature digital banking capabilities or mobile app details.
services: Personal checking accounts (free personal checking advertised)
Credit cards with rewards and cash back options
Auto loans for purchase and refinance
Financial literacy resources (emergency fund creation, scam avoidance, vishing awareness)
Member-exclusive event sponsorships and partner discounts
Local business partnership programs with member incentives
Board of Directors member voting and governance participation
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means lower interest rates and fewer fees than traditional banks
Over 75 years of local history and deep roots in Douglas County community
Significant annual community investment: $40K+ to schools, $70K+ to events, $40K+ to agricultural programs
Exclusive member perks including free Wildlife Safari admission and Festival of Lights discounts
Ranked #1 credit union in Oregon and #3 in the United States
Full-service financial products from checking accounts to mortgages
Competitive auto loan rates with partnerships at local dealerships
cons: Membership restricted to Douglas County residents, those who work/worship in the area, or specific nearby towns—not available nationally
Limited information on website about digital banking, mobile app features, or online-only account opening capabilities
No mention of alternative financial products like personal loans, debt consolidation, or credit-building services
Website lacks detailed rate comparison data or specific APR/term examples for major products
No indication of 24/7 customer support or expanded service hours beyond traditional credit union operations
best_for: Douglas County residents and workers seeking community-focused banking with local accountability
Individuals looking to refinance auto loans through a credit union with local dealer partnerships
Community-minded consumers who prioritize supporting local institutions and educational initiatives
Families and farmers in rural Oregon wanting to support agricultural programs (4H and FFA)
diagnosis: Cascade Community Credit Union is best for Douglas County residents and workers seeking member-owned banking with strong local community investment and competitive rates. The main caveat is strict geographic membership restrictions and a lack of clarity around digital banking capabilities, making it less suitable for consumers outside the service area or those prioritizing online-first financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Cascade — Credit Union in Kent, WA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Cascade — NCUA-insured credit union in Kent, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
